title: [HW] Parameterized modules, step #1
body:
I'd recommend that Step 1 for us to represent parameters in an explicit way on HWModule*Op.  Right now, we have parameters on hw.instance, but not on the declarations.

This looks like:

1) Encode the parameter names and their types (I'd recommend starting with just integers) on HWModule*Op, put them in a linear order that can be indexed by an integer.
2) Change `$parameters` on InstanceOp to be a (non-optional) array of values, instead of a key/value store.  Assert in the verifier that the type of the value matches the declared type in the HWModule declaration, just like we check that ports line up.
3) Change the verilog printer to print parameters in the new encoding.

This won't add any significant new capabilities, but is a stepping stone.  For Step 2 I'd recommend allowing a parameter to an HWModuleOp to be passable into an HWInstance, which will require start paving the "how do we refer to attributes as values in a module" path.
